mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS series signals easier discounting and higher equity risk appetite, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive when the policy rate is drifting lower from already-restrictive levels.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or indistinguishable from baseline in periods where FEDFUNDS declines for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a short-run threshold marks labor-market deterioration that precedes weaker corporate earnings expectations and lower equity prices, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broad demand expansion in employment and retail spending, which supports near-term revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS-RSAFS improvement does not outperform either series alone in out-of-sample forecasting, or if the triggered 60-day forward return distribution is not shifted upward versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO after a prior high signals an industrial slowdown that reduces cyclical profit expectations, so S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days are negative when industrial production momentum turns down sharply.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls for 2 consecutive monthly releases and the 3-month change is below 0
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are positive in a majority of instances following the specified INDPRO deterioration, or if the signal's hit rate is not better than 50% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains below 1% while CPIAUCSL inflation is rising, real rates stay compressed and financial conditions are loose, which supports higher S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 1.0% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation is positive
failure_condition: Reject if low-rate inflationary regimes do not show higher 60-day forward SPX returns than comparable low-rate deflationary regimes, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value versus FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST signals strengthening housing demand and construction activity, which leads broader economic activity and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising HOUST does not precede above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal turns negative during at least two consecutive years of walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, labor income momentum weakens and equity investors reprice earnings lower, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth turns negative while UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined deterioration does not outperform single-variable PAYEMS or UNRATE signals, or if triggered events produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-led cooling regime, weakening INDPRO together with flat or falling RSAFS indicates both production and sales softening, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns remain positive during periods of concurrent INDPRO and RSAFS softness, or if the joint signal fails to beat a random benchmark over any 3-year rolling window.
